brand.name: Catena
brand.tagline: Managed IT services for Quebec SMBs.

banner.message: Now booking founding clients. Personal onboarding, direct line.

notfound.title: Page not found
notfound.headline: That page doesn't exist.
notfound.body: The link may be stale or the page has moved. Here are a few useful starting points:

hero.headline: Your apps. Your data. Our expertise.
hero.subheadline: Email, files, chat, CRM, scheduling, billing -- a managed open-source suite on infrastructure that stays in your name. We pick tools that age well, run them with real backup drills and version discipline, and hand you the keys any time.

bio.body: Run by Marc-Antoine Lalonde, based in Quebec.

dogfood.title: We're our first client.
dogfood.body: Everything you see -- this site, the docs, the client portal, billing, our incident dashboards -- runs on the same Catena Suite we sell. Every update you install has run on our own deployment first.

upstream.label: Built on open-source projects you may already know

sovereignty.title: Sovereignty, in plain language
sovereignty.intro: You own the assets. We operate them. What that means in practice.
sovereignty.vps_body: Your provider, your account, your invoice. We operate; you own.
sovereignty.dns_body: DNS records under your name. We wire them up; we never hold them.
sovereignty.backups_body: Encrypted, immutable nightly snapshots to your own S3 storage. You hold the keys.
sovereignty.exit_body: No vendor lock-in. Leave with your data at any time in one click.

onestop.title: The Catena Suite. One contact. One invoice.
onestop.intro: All the capabilities your team needs to run the business, integrated and operated end-to-end.

talk.title: Talk to your team and your customers

onestop.email_note: Email is integrated, not self-hosted -- 5 full-featured, privacy-focused providers to choose from based on your needs. We wire up DNS, deliverability, and SSO.

features.title: Features
features.intro: What your team uses every day, and what runs underneath it. Two sections: the apps your team interacts with, and the technical surface for the IT person evaluating the suite.
features.extra_apps_title: Looking for something we don't ship?
features.extra_apps_footnote: Anything not on our vetted list is a custom-template request -- talk to us.
features.technical_link_body: The technical surface (Ansible, Tailscale, Cloudflare Tunnel, Keycloak, SOPS, Restic, Dokploy, monitoring) lives on its own page so the everyday-apps view stays focused.

tool_comparison.title: How the Catena Suite picks compare
tool_comparison.intro: Side-by-side with the SaaS bundles most SMBs evaluate. The Catena Suite picks are open-source, single-tenant, and run on infrastructure you own.

for_your_team.intro: What your team uses every day. Single sign-on across the whole suite -- one password to remember, no more separate logins per tool.
for_your_team.recommendation_body: Each capability on this list is delivered by a vetted open-source tool that earned its slot on feature coverage, stability, project history, and popularity. Find an app that fits your needs or replaces what you currently pay for among hundreds of others at openalternative.co.

communications.title: Integrated communications
communications.intro: Internal team chat, video meetings, customer-support inbox, and regular phone calls. Plays the role Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Zoom play at most shops. No single tool covers everything; the linked guide walks through which combination fits which situation.

files_and_workspace.title: Files and shared workspace
files_and_workspace.intro: Your team's shared drive. Open-source equivalent of SharePoint, OneDrive, or Dropbox. Desktop and mobile sync, version history, shared folders, link sharing with expiry. Files stay on your server, not on a third party. Nextcloud also bundles basic in-app tools for chat, wiki, calendar, contacts, notes, and tasks -- enough for small teams before adding the dedicated apps in the suite.
files_and_workspace.cta_label: Compare Nextcloud apps vs the suite

collaborative_editing.title: Collaborative document editing
collaborative_editing.intro: Edit documents, spreadsheets, and presentations together in the browser, plugged into Nextcloud so your team edits files in place. Pick OnlyOffice if your team works mainly in Microsoft formats (.docx / .xlsx / .pptx) -- it has the highest fidelity for those. Pick Collabora if your team uses or is ready to move to open formats (.odt / .ods / .odp) -- LibreOffice-based and the natural home for ODF.

team_documentation.title: Team documentation
team_documentation.intro: A wiki for your team's processes, policies, and how-to notes. Open-source alternative to Notion or Confluence. Better than scattered shared folders and Google Docs nobody can find.

document_signing.title: Document signing and audit trail
document_signing.intro: Send documents for electronic signature with audit trail, certificate of completion, and reusable templates. Self-hosted alternative to DocuSign.

crm.title: CRM and customer follow-up
crm.intro: Contacts, opportunities, follow-ups, per-member calendars, team coordination. Open-source alternative to HubSpot, Salesforce, or Pipedrive. Light enough to use day-to-day, deep enough to grow into. Single sign-on with the rest of the suite.

booking.title: Client booking and scheduling
booking.intro: Customer-facing booking page for one or many providers. Open-source alternative to Calendly or Acuity. Staff calendars sync with Nextcloud. Stripe-paid bookings for clinics, salons, lessons. Different teams have different scheduler needs -- the linked picker points you at the right one.

invoicing.title: Quotes and invoices
invoicing.intro: Quotes, invoices, recurring billing, expense tracking, online payments via Stripe / PayPal, and a client portal. Self-hosted alternative to FreshBooks or Wave.

workflow_automation.title: Workflow automation
workflow_automation.intro: Connect the dots. Open-source alternative to Zapier or Make. When a form comes in, create an EspoCRM contact, send a confirmation email, schedule a follow-up -- without copy-paste.

project_management.title: Project planning and tracking
project_management.intro: Issues, cycles, modules, projects, and roadmaps. Open-source alternative to Jira and Linear, with a clean modern UI and a sane data model.

social_posting.title: Omnichannel social posting
social_posting.intro: Schedule posts to LinkedIn, X, Facebook, and Instagram from one calendar. Open-source alternative to Buffer or Hootsuite.

website_hosting.title: Marketing website hosting
website_hosting.intro: A multi-page company website for your business, deployed on the same suite as everything else. Source code in a Git repo under your name; bundled options on the pricing page.

email_integration.title: Email integration
email_integration.intro: We don't run a mail server -- delivery, spam compliance, and bounce handling are a full-time job. We integrate with five privacy-focused providers and wire up MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and single sign-on. Your team uses email like any other app.

for_your_it.intro: The technical surface, for the IT person evaluating whether this suite is something they want us to run, or something they could run in-house. Five first-class capabilities -- secure deployment, secure access, backup and recovery, application orchestration, observability -- each one delivered by a small set of well-known open-source tools.

secure_deployment.title: Secure deployment
secure_deployment.intro: Every server gets the same hardened baseline. Ansible runs the deployment automation; SOPS encrypts secrets in Git with age, decrypted only at deploy time. No secret ever lands on disk in plaintext outside our hardware-keyed password manager. Add a recipient; rotate cleanly.

secure_access.title: Secure access
secure_access.intro: Three layers of access control. Cloudflare Tunnel terminates public TLS at the edge so the host has zero open ports (80/443 stay closed, no router configuration needed). Tailscale carries our admin path on a private mesh, and public SSH closes after bootstrap. Keycloak handles per-application identity (users, groups, password policy, MFA) so every app in the suite shares one login; oauth2-proxy fronts non-OIDC apps via Traefik forward-auth.

backup_recovery.title: Backup and recovery
backup_recovery.intro: Encrypted nightly backups to your own immutable S3 storage for ransomware protection. Restore and verify flows are first-class, and quarterly drills run on a schedule -- a real fresh-VPS round-trip proves the no-lock-in claim.

orchestration.title: Application orchestration
orchestration.intro: Compose-style application deployments managed via Dokploy's UI on the tailnet. Containers update with health-baseline rollback on regression.

observability.title: Observability
observability.intro: Two separate monitoring vantage points. Gatus probes HTTP endpoints from inside and outside the network. Self-hosted Healthchecks does dead-man monitoring on every scheduled job. Each can fail without the other going dark.

for_your_it.architecture_link: How the whole suite fits together

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.intro: Transparent, sovereignty-first pricing. Pay per server, not per seat. Three line items: the server, the apps you run on it, and the support you actually use. Resize any one without renegotiating a tier. All prices in Canadian dollars (CAD).

server.name: Server
server.tagline: The hardened base. One server, one monthly fee, unlimited team members. Pick which apps run on top.
features: Cloudflare Tunnel + Tailscale + Keycloak SSO baseline
Encrypted nightly backups to your own S3, drilled quarterly
4 business hour response SLA, no included hours

apps.name: Apps
apps.tagline: Every app you run on the server.
features: Upgrade testing on our own deployment first
Vetted-catalog apps install at no extra setup cost

support.name: Support
support.tagline: Pick a pack or pay as you go.
features: Non-deferrable; overage bills at the daytime rate

pricing.hosting_note: Hosting and backups are passthrough. The OVH VPS and the eazybackup S3 bucket land on your invoice, in your name, at the vendor's published price. Catena does not markup infrastructure.
pricing.examples_intro: Four common compositions to anchor expectations. Mix and match to fit your team.

light.name: Light
light.components: Server + 2 apps + ad-hoc support (~1 hour / month)

cabinet.name: Cabinet
cabinet.tagline: Professional services with deep integration and high responsiveness.

pricing.commitment_note: Managed subscription carries a 6-month minimum commitment. After that, cancel any month with no exit fee. We hand back the suite and your S3 export per the engagement letter.
pricing.alacarte_support_note: Hours outside your support pack bill at the time-of-day rate grid below.

sovereignty.no_per_seat_body: One monthly fee per server. Add unlimited team members at no extra cost.
sovereignty.ownership_body: The server, the domain, the backup bucket, the data -- all in your name and your accounts.
sovereignty.privacy_body: Built for Law 25 and PIPEDA. Architected for GDPR-style residency and portability.

scope.title: What's included and what isn't
scope.intro: The monthly retainer covers the Catena Suite we run for you. For on-site hardware or tools we don't host, you'll need a local technician.
included: Support for all apps in your Catena Suite (Nextcloud, EspoCRM, Easy!Appointments, etc.)
Incident response per the engagement-letter SLA
not_included: Microsoft 365, Google Workspace (not hosted by us)
New features or new apps (project quote)

alacarte.title: Hours outside the retainer
alacarte.intro: For work that falls outside your monthly retainer but touches your Catena Suite. Billed in quarter-hour increments, 15-minute minimum.
alacarte.projects_note: New features, new apps, and website redesigns are quoted separately as fixed-price projects before work starts.

migration.title: Data migration
migration.intro: Leaving Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Dropbox, HubSpot, or another tool? We build the bridge.
migration.body: Quoted individually based on volume and complexity. Fixed quote after a 1-2h discovery (included, no commitment). Historical range: $500 to $5,000+. Covers DNS / MX cutover, IMAP imports via imapsync, rclone for files, CSV / API exports for CRM, schema mapping, and post-migration verification.

compliance.title: Law 25 compliance
compliance.intro: The Catena Suite is Law 25 compliant at the infrastructure level: Canadian residency, documented subprocessors, immutable backups, MFA, encryption, logging.
compliance.body: For the documentation layer (PIA / EFVP, registers, policies, incident runbook, audit), we offer separate compliance enablement. We do not replace your internal privacy officer -- Law 25 requires this to be a person inside your organization. We deliver the technical infrastructure and documentation your privacy officer can adopt and sign.
compliance.build_notice: Law 25 audit and documentation packages in build (under legal review). See our own application of the framework at /en/conformite. Talk to us to reserve a slot or discuss your situation.

addons.title: Add-ons
addons.intro: Optional services billed alongside your monthly retainer.

website.name: Marketing website
website.description: Multi-page, high-performance company website, deployed as an app on the same server as the rest of your Catena Suite. Transparent itemized pricing:
website.base_label: Base site (5 pages, EN or FR)
website.embeds_label: Standard embeds (Easy!Appointments, EspoCRM lead form, etc.)
website.financing: Pay upfront OR spread over 6 monthly installments alongside your monthly retainer (financing is only available to clients on an active retainer, minimum 6 months).
website.scope_note: Custom integrations (custom UI on top of APIs, bespoke workflows) are separate projects -- see Hours outside the retainer for hourly rates, or ask for a project quote.
website.note: E-commerce websites not included. For a deeper cabinet-tier site (10+ pages bilingual + custom design), talk to us.

scheduler_picker.title: Find your scheduler
scheduler_picker.intro: Scheduling needs vary too much for a single default. Answer a few quick questions and we'll point you at the right app from the catalog.

comparison.title: Comparison
comparison.intro: Honest read on where Catena fits versus the alternatives most SMBs evaluate. The dominant axis is sovereignty: who owns the data, the infrastructure, the keys.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: The fastest way to reach us is a 30-minute discovery call. We do not pitch on first contact -- we ask questions and work out together whether Catena is the right fit.
contact.discovery_body: Thirty minutes, no commitment. We listen, you decide whether anything we offer matches your situation. Bring questions, not a brief.
contact.response_body: We aim to reply within one business day.

form.title: Send a message
form.intro: Or fill in the form below. Replies come from the same inbox.
form.intent_label: What are you looking for? (pick all that apply)
form.intent_managed: A managed software suite for my company

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: The questions prospects ask most often before booking a discovery call. Short answers; the longer story is in About / Pricing / Comparison.

q1.question: Does Catena run on the Catena Suite?
q1.answer: Same suite. Same hands. Live since launch. catena.run, the docs at /docs, the client portal, the external healthchecks for client services, payment processing, and invoicing all run on the Catena Suite we sell. The whole revenue surface is on the product. Dogfooding is the warranty.

q2.question: What else can I run on the suite?
q2.answer: Anything that fits a Dokploy compose template. Our vetted catalog (Nextcloud, OnlyOffice / Collabora, Rocket.Chat, Plane, DocuSeal, Invoice Ninja, Outline, EspoCRM, n8n, Postiz, Twenty, WordPress, ERPNext, Easy!Appointments, and others) installs at no extra setup cost. Anything else is a custom-template request -- talk to us. To explore the broader self-hosted ecosystem: awesome-selfhosted, awweso.me, Dokploy templates, awesome-sysadmin.

q3.question: What happens if you get hit by a bus?
q3.answer: The whole suite is open-source and portable. Another competent IT partner can pick it up using the same automation.

q4.question: Can I leave with my data?
q4.answer: You don't have to leave -- you already own everything. The server, the data, and the backup buckets are all in your name. When you want to part ways, we hand over and step out. No exit fees, no proprietary glue to unwind.

q5.question: Do I need to be technical to use this?
q5.answer: No. Daily use is browser-based (Nextcloud / Rocket.Chat / etc.). The technical surface is what we operate on your behalf -- your team only ever sees apps.

q6.question: Where is my data physically located?
q6.answer: On the server you own, at the provider you chose. Default deployment: OVH BHS (Beauharnois, Quebec) for the live server + hot S3 mirror; eazybackup (Canadian-owned, datacenter in Ottawa, Canada -- Canadian government-grade certified, with Object Lock + versioning) for the cold mirror. Both Canadian-jurisdiction, Law-25-friendly.

q7.question: How do you make money?
q7.answer: Monthly subscription per server, plus per-app and an optional support pack. No per-seat, ever. Hosting and backups are passthrough to your own accounts. See the Pricing page.

q8.question: Do you self-host email?
q8.answer: No -- email delivery is a full-time specialty (deliverability, spam compliance, bounce handling). We integrate with five privacy-focused providers and wire up DNS + SSO so your team uses email like any other app. Suggested clients: Nextcloud's built-in mail interface (browser-based), Thunderbird (desktop, free, cross-platform), or Betterbird (Thunderbird fork with extra polish).

q9.question: Can I host my own apps alongside the suite?
q9.answer: Yes. Dokploy lets you deploy any compose-shaped app under SSO + Cloudflare Tunnel. We can advise; we don't have to operate it.

q11.question: Can I run this on my own hardware?
q11.answer: Yes. Self-hosted installs on hardware you own (with or without a public IP) are available as a custom install. Talk to us to discuss the constraints and quote.

q12.question: What currency are your prices in?
q12.answer: Canadian dollars (CAD). Cross-border prospects pay via Stripe in CAD; Stripe handles the conversion at their interbank rate at billing time.

q13.question: What's the difference between Catena and the Catena Suite?
q13.answer: Catena is the company (the MSP). The Catena Suite is the software Catena operates for you: Nextcloud, EspoCRM, Easy!Appointments, Rocket.Chat, Keycloak, and the Ansible / backups / monitoring tooling that runs the whole thing. Your invoice comes from Catena; your email and files live on the Catena Suite.

q14.question: Are you really an MSP? How are you different from my current MSP?
q14.answer: Yes, Catena is an MSP -- we run IT infrastructure on your behalf for a monthly retainer. The difference from a traditional MSP: our scope is bounded to the Catena Suite. We do not manage your workstations, printers, Microsoft 365, or office network (see the Included / Not included grid). If you need help with anything beyond the suite, talk to us. In exchange: you pay per server (not per seat), your code and data belong to you, and you can leave at any time with everything in hand.

q15.question: What can you produce as Law 25 documentation?
q15.answer: The Catena Suite is Law 25 compliant at the infrastructure level (Canadian residency, documented subprocessors, immutable backups, MFA, encryption, logging). For the documentation layer (PIA / EFVP, registers, policies, incident runbook), we offer separate compliance enablement: audit and initial pack (in build, under legal review). Important: we do not replace your internal privacy officer -- Law 25 requires this to be a person inside your organization -- we deliver the infrastructure and documents that person can adopt and sign. See the Law 25 section.

about.title: About
about.intro: Catena is led by Marc-Antoine Lalonde. Here is what that means in practice.
about.bus_factor_note: Worried about a one-person business? The whole suite is open-source and portable -- another competent IT partner can pick up your suite using the same automation.
about.story_body: Thirteen years of technical leadership across software, infrastructure work and systems integration. Five years solo, building and running my companies' suites. Catena is what I am best at and what I most enjoy: picking the right tools, knowing how they fail, and operating them end to end on the Catena Suite. Quebec-based; bilingual delivery.
about.bring_judgment_body: Picking tools is not the commodity part of this work. Knowing which open-source projects are healthy, which will be maintained, and which will silently break in two years -- that is the differentiator.
about.bring_failure_body: I design backwards from the recovery scenarios. Disaster recovery, rollbacks, migration, decommission. The day a thing breaks is not the day to learn how to fix it.
about.bring_ownership_body: From provisioning to backups to migration to handover, one set of unified tools. No excuses, no deferral, only accountability.
about.work_response_body: Standard tier: 4 business hours. Emergency service available. Basic set of "panic buttons" in your server so you can take over in the meantime.
about.work_cadence_body: Quarterly review call. Monthly summary email of what was bumped, restored, and validated on your suite. Managed backup-restore drills and the report lands in your inbox.
about.work_continuity_body: Nothing in your suite is closed-source or proprietary to me. The whole suite is open-source and portable; another competent IT partner can pick up your suite using the same automation.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: Catena does not share, sell, rent, or train AI on your data. The whole point of the suite is that you own it.
privacy.collect_body: Billing information (via Stripe -- card numbers never touch our servers). Portal account information (email, name, company). Credentials you enter to deploy your suite are passed in-flight to our deployment runner and are not persisted in our database. Server-side logs of our actions for support and audit.
privacy.dont_body: We do not sell, rent, or share your data. We do not train AI models on it. We do not analyze it for advertising. We do not use third-party trackers on the marketing site or the portal.
privacy.where_body: Your suite data lives on the server you own, the S3 bucket you own, and the third-party accounts you yourself selected (Cloudflare, Tailscale, your VPS provider, your SMTP provider, etc.). The portal itself runs on Canadian-jurisdiction infrastructure and is hosted on the Catena Suite -- the same product we sell.
privacy.subprocessors_body: Stripe (payment processing). Cloudflare (Tunnel + edge for catena.run + the portal). Keycloak (identity, on our infrastructure). The providers you yourself selected for your suite (named in your engagement letter).
privacy.rights_body: Law 25 and PIPEDA grant you the right to access, correct, and delete personal information we hold. Contact privacy@catena.run (or hello@catena.run interim) and we will respond within 30 days.
privacy.cookies_body: Minimal session cookies on the portal for authentication. No third-party trackers, no advertising cookies, no cookies on the marketing site. Aggregate visit analytics on the marketing site via a self-hosted Plausible instance -- no cookies, no fingerprinting, no personal data; Law 25 / GDPR compliant with no consent banner required.
privacy.contact_body: Email hello@catena.run. We answer privacy questions within one business day.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.intro: These terms cover the relationship between Catena (the service provider) and you (the client). They are deliberately concise -- the engagement letter you sign at signup is the canonical contract; this page summarizes its main commitments.
terms.review_notice: This page is a draft pending legal review. Talk to us directly for the binding terms in your engagement letter.
terms.scope_body: Your subscription components -- server, apps, and support pack -- define what is included. See the Pricing page for the public model. Changes to scope (adding or removing apps, support pack changes) are agreed in writing.
terms.billing_body: The managed subscription (server + apps + support pack) carries a 6-month minimum commitment, billed monthly. À-la-carte support is billed at the published rate in 15-minute increments. Card processing via Stripe.
terms.ownership_body: All client data, infrastructure, and credentials belong to the client. Our role is service provider only. You can export and migrate at any time; the engagement letter documents the handover process.
terms.liability_body: Liability is capped at amounts proportionate to the most recent monthly retainer; the binding limits are set out in your engagement letter.
terms.sla_body: Target uptime, response times, and recovery objectives for restore drills are set out in your engagement letter and reviewed at the quarterly check-in.
terms.aup_body: No illegal content, no unsolicited bulk email (spam), no malware distribution, no resale of the suite as-a-service to third parties without prior agreement. Standard internet-services posture.
terms.termination_body: Either party may terminate per the engagement letter. On termination, we hand over the suite and the client's S3 export; data retained on Catena-owned systems is purged within 30 days.
terms.perservice_body: Each upstream app in the suite carries its own license and acceptable-use policy (Nextcloud GPL, Rocket.Chat MIT, OnlyOffice AGPL, etc.). The Project page link on /features points to each upstream's official terms.
terms.law_body: Quebec, Canada. Disputes go to the courts of the judicial district of Montreal.

portal.signin_body: Sign in with the Keycloak (SSO) account set up for you. New here? Ask your Catena contact for an invite.

site_web.title: Marketing website
site_web.intro: A multi-page website, deployed as an app on the same server as your Catena Suite. You own the source code (a Git repo under your name), the content, and the domain. No vendor lock-in, no third-party host to pay for.
site_web.why_title: Why design your website with Catena
site_web.why_intro: Anyone can launch a website this year -- AI builders will hand you one in an afternoon. The real question is what happens later, when the platform raises its prices, shuts down, decides your industry violates its terms, or you business grows. Four reasons a Catena-built site holds up.
site_web.why_card1_body: Source code in a Git repository under your name. Content in plain markdown anyone can read. Swap hosts in five minutes by changing a single Astro adapter. The site is a folder of files you own, not a row in someone else's database.
site_web.why_card2_body: Booking embeds that know who is logged in. Contact forms that drop straight into your CRM. Member areas gated by your SSO. AI builders cannot stitch this together -- they do not run the systems on the other end. Your Catena Suite does.
site_web.why_card3_body: When the platform doubles its prices, gets acquired, decides your payment processor is no longer allowed, or goes down for eighteen hours, the answer should not be a support ticket. With a Catena site, the answer is a redeploy: same files, different host, five minutes.
site_web.why_card4_body: Leads go to your CRM. Bookings go to your booking system. Logins go to your identity server. The site itself owns nothing, so there is nothing on it to back up, migrate, or lose. State lives where it already belongs.
site_web.why_tradeoff_title: When it is the wrong fit
site_web.why_tradeoff_body: If you need a single brochure page and will never touch it again, a no-code builder is genuinely a good tool for the job. Talk to us when the site is part of how your business actually runs -- the front door to your bookings, your CRM, your portal, and your team.
site_web.process_step1_body: You explain your business, your audience, your goals. I explain what I offer and what is or isn't realistic in your budget. No pitch, no commitment.
site_web.process_step2_body: You fill out a multi-step form in the client portal: business name, logo, colors, desired pages, target audience, reference sites you love and hate, existing content. Auto-saves; come back any time.
site_web.process_step3_body: Itemized quote in CAD per the price grid below. No hidden costs. You approve before work starts.
site_web.process_step4_body: Mockup -> content -> Astro static build -> review -> revisions. Two rounds of revisions included. You see the site on a preview URL throughout the build.
site_web.process_step5_body: Going live on your Catena Suite, DNS cutover, HTTPS / accessibility / performance verification. Source code handed over in a Git repo under your name.
site_web.process_step6_body: Small content edits (< 1h / month) are included in your retainer. Beyond that, see Hours outside the retainer. A full redesign is a separate project quote.
site_web.pricing_intro: Transparent itemized pricing, no hidden fees. The examples show the most common combinations.
deliverables: High-performance static Astro site (TTFB < 200 ms, Lighthouse 95+)
Source code in a Git repo under your name (portable elsewhere at any time)
Schema.org for local SEO (address, hours, services)
Bilingual Law-25-compliant privacy policy (if you purchase bilingual)
Open Graph metadata for social sharing
Hosted on your Catena Suite (no third-party host to pay for)
not_included: Content creation (copy, photography) -- you provide it, or I refer you to a copywriter / photographer
E-commerce sites with shopping cart and payment gateway (separate project quote)
Custom integrations beyond standard embeds (separate project quote)
Active SEO beyond initial setup (audit + keyword research + monthly content: separate project quote)
Redesign of an existing site after delivery (separate project quote)
site_web.cta_body: The fastest way is a 30-minute discovery call. No pitch, no commitment.

app_selector.title: App selector
app_selector.intro: Tick the business needs that apply to you, then tick apps to cover them. The bill on the right updates live; gaps and overlaps are flagged inline. Mix Catena-managed apps with external SaaS to compare suites side by side.
app_selector.meta_description: Map your business needs to the right apps -- Catena-managed, included with the server, or external SaaS. See a live monthly cost and compare a Catena suite to QuickBooks-only or Microsoft 365-only setups.

inputs.employees_help: Drives per-seat external SaaS pricing. Catena pricing is per server + per app; never per seat.
inputs.storage_help: Approximate Catena backup overhead at $0.10 CAD/GB/month.

app_warnings.erpnext_not_recommended: Not recommended for new clients. Compliance and payment gaps in the Canadian SMB use case; kept in catalog for specialized needs only.

status.gap: No selected app covers this need.

bill.empty_hint: Tick a need or an app to start pricing.
bill.tier_user_cap_exceeded: {users} exceeds {cap}-user cap -- pick a higher tier
